{{CargoRelic
|name=Sonnet
|rank=Blue
|affct=CIV
|birthday=09-18
|source=Lottery Machine (0.03%), Wishing (0.82%)
|description=On Sep 18, 1820, Shelley wrote the well-recognized best collection of English poems. 

The King had to release the patriots to commemorate the Spanish uprising. Shelly wrote this 14-line poem in response to this event. Neat and rhythmic, this poem expressed the beauty of love. It is well-loved among the literati, including Shakespeare. Humanism, including the pursuit of freedom, equality, and charity, is a viral value among the aristocrats.

[Classic Quotes]

The more we study, the more we discover our ignorance. If winter comes, can spring be far behind? Only life could be destroyed, not space.
|fame=22
|art=27
|fth=30
|civ=44
|tech=31
|ss1=In explorations, +0.4% chance to gain 2x INTEL
|ss2=In explorations, +0.8% chance to gain 2x INTEL
|ss3=In explorations, +1.2% chance to gain 2x INTEL
|ss4=In explorations, +2% chance to gain 2x INTEL
|ss7=In explorations, +2% chance to gain 2x INTEL<br>In explorations, +2% chance to gain 2x INTEL (Awakened)
|skill1=[[File:Genesim skill I.png|50px]] Place in Gene Sim Device: Snail HP +200
}}

[[Category:Relics]]
[[Category:Blue Relics]]
[[Category:CIV Relics]]
[[Category:Blue CIV Relics]]
[[Category:DEF Boost]]
[[Category:ATK Boost]]
[[Category:Gene Simulation Compass Boost]]
[[Category:Exploration INTEL Boost]]
